SPEECH.SPEAKER: Lian

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Lian
SPEECH.LINE: I'd be so happy if ours was that fancy cause then...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Why is that? 'Cause is looks better? This boat has seen us through many storms. It may not look like much but, it has something theirs never will

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: Are the children learning something, dear?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: It is unclear. All right, sit, children. I think it's time that I told you the story of Aladdin, the Princess, and the lamp.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Lian
SPEECH.LINE: What's so special about a lamp?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, this is a magic lamp.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: No, no. No singing. It's been a long day.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Where you wander among every culture and tongue
It's chaotic, but hey, it's home.
When the wind's from the east and the sun's from the west
And the sand in the glass is right
Come on down, stop on by

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: As you wind through the streets
Of the silks and the satin shawls
As you move through a maze
In the haze of your pure delight
You are caught in a dance
You are lost in the trance
Are hotter than hot In a lot of good ways
There’s a road that may lead you
To good or to greed through
Well, your destiny lies in your hands

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Cave Of Wonders
SPEECH.LINE: Only one may enter here. One whose worth lies far within. A diamond in the rough.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Cave Of Wonders
SPEECH.LINE: Seek me out. The diamond in the rough.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Women 1

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: He, uh... Oh! I think this... belongs to me. Good day, ladies. Okay. How'd we do, Abu? Good monkey.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Whoa, whoa, I didn't steal anything. It's a family heirloom that's worth a lot of...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Zulla
SPEECH.LINE: I'll give you a bag of dates for it, nothing more.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Zulla, we both know it's worth at least three bags.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Zulla
SPEECH.LINE: Take a bag of dates and get out!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jamal
SPEECH.LINE: Hey. Hey! You steal from my brother.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jamal
SPEECH.LINE: You pay or I take bracelet.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Sir, I don't have any money. Let go of me! No.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jamal
SPEECH.LINE: Khalil walks away from the stall, and this one... this one... she steal the bread!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jamal
SPEECH.LINE: You keep your little street rat nose out of it! Huh?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Okay. Trust me. Here you go.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: This is what you wanted, right?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Okay, and an apple for your troubles. Nice. -

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: That was m... I'm not leaving without my bracelet!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You mean, this bracelet? Come on.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jamal
SPEECH.LINE: Nay nay nay na-na-nay Nay nay nay na-na-nay Nay nay nay nay nay... Aladdin! Thief, Aladdin!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Down that alley. Monkey knows the way. Abu. Cute butt. You'll be fine. -

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: One jump ahead of the bread line
One swing ahead of the sword
I steal only what I can't afford.
One jump ahead of the lawmen
That's all, and that's no joke
These guys don't appreciate I'm broke

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Take that! Try a different tact, guys

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Women

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Woman

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Gotta eat to live, gotta steal to eat Tell you all about it when I got the time Hey!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: One jump ahead of the slowpokes
One skip ahead of my doom
Next time gonna use a nom de plume
One jump ahead of the hitmen
One hit ahead of the flock
I think I'll take a stroll around the block

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Lady
SPEECH.LINE: Still I think he's rather tasty

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Gotta eat to live, gotta steal to eat Otherwise we'd get along

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Why are you repeating everything I say? The pole. One, two, three!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Look at me. Look at me. You... can do this.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: One jump ahead of the hoofbeats
One hop ahead of the hump
They're quick, but I'm much faster
All I gotta do is jump!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Come on. I know somewhere we'll be safe.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: You bring me the rough but never a diamond. Take them away.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Prisoner
SPEECH.LINE: Please! Majazeer, perhaps this diamond in the rough does not exist.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Prisoner
SPEECH.LINE: But we've searched for months. I do not understand what could possibly be in that cave that could help a man as great as you. You are already second only to the Sultan.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Iago
SPEECH.LINE: Second! Uh-oh. Who's in trouble now?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: And you think second is enough?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Prisoner
SPEECH.LINE: Of course. You were not born to be sultan.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Do you know what I had to do to get the power that I have? The sacrifices that I've made? The bodies that I've buried? The five years spent in a Sherabad jail? People need to understand that they will pay for underestimating me. Second is not enough! And it will never be enough! That's why I need the lamp. And why I no longer need you!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, my... Is this.. is this where you live?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Yep. Just me and Abu. Come and go as we please. Welcome to my humble... You'll see.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: I can't believe we did that. That I did that. That we are alive! With the chase.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Thank you. And thank you for getting me out of there. Aladdin, isn't it?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Well, only someone from the palace could afford a bracelet like that. And that silk lining is imported, too. It comes from the merchant boats straight to the palace. But not to servants. At least not most servants. Which means you... are a handmaid to the Princess.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You think that's impressive. You should see the city from up there.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Agrabah. It's so beautiful. I should get out more.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You should tell the Princess to get out more. The people haven't seen her in years.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: They won't let her. Ever since my... The Queen was killed, the Sultan's been afraid. So she's kept locked away.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Seems everyone's been afraid since then. But the people of Agrabah had nothing to do with that. The people loved her.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: They did, didn't they? Is this yours?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: It's kind of... borrowed. My mother taught me that song.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: It's all I remember of her.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I lost them both when I was young. Been on my own ever since. It's all right, it's just...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: It's a little sad... having a monkey as the only parental authority in your life. We get by. Everyday, I just... think things will be different, but it never seems to change. Just sometimes, I feel like I'm...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Trapped. Like you... can't escape what you were born into?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: I have to get back to the palace.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: MAN 1
SPEECH.LINE: Make way for Prince Anders! Make way for Prince Anders!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: It's just another prince coming to court the princess.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Yes, and I have to get her ready. Oh, do you have my bracelet?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Sure. I'm sure I put it in here. Somewhere. Abu, did you take it?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: I was so naive. Excuse me.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Wait. Wait. Wait! Wait, it's not like that!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Guard
SPEECH.LINE: Street rat, get out of the way!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Who do you think you're calling a street rat?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Guard
SPEECH.LINE: Are you talking back to me? You were born worthless and you will die worthless. And only your fleas will mourn you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Come on, Abu. Let's go home.

SCENE.TITLE: SCENE 10
SCENE.STAGEDIR: Start song: One Jump Ahead Reprise

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Would they see a poor boy?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Abu. There's a time that we do steal, and a time we don't. This was definitely a don't.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Welcome, Prince Anders. We trust you had an enjoyable journey.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Prince Anders, my daughter, Princess Jasmine.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Prince Anders
SPEECH.LINE: S-So... Why did no one tell me of your beauty?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Prince Anders
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, thank you. They say that in Skinland. Ya, right? It's... It's very, very amusing

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: We have that same title yet arenever described the same way.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Prince Anders
SPEECH.LINE: Ya. Oh. What is that? Don't. Don't tell me. It's a cat... with stripes.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Prince Anders
SPEECH.LINE: I know.Cause in Sknland, cats love me. Here, puss-puss. Hey, puss-puss. Hey, puss-puss, puss...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: My Sultan... Our enemies grow stronger everyday, yet you allow your daughterto dismiss Prince Anders and a possible military alliance.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: You would drag us into a war with our only...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Yet you would allow your kingdom to sink into ruin, for mere sentiment!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: I apologize. Forgive me, my Sultan. I went too far. But... if you would only reconsider, I think you would see that invading Sherabad is the right thing to do.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Why would we invade Mother's kingdom?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: But an ally in Skanland would improve our situation.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Yes. If you consider giving Prince Anders a chance.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: To rule? Baba, Rajah would make a better ruler.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: My dear, I'm not getting any younger. We must find you a husband and... we are running out of kingdoms.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: What foreign prince can care for our people as I do? I could lead, if only...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: My dear, you cannot be a sultan. Because it has never been done in the thousand-year history of our kingdom.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: I have been preparing for this my whole life. I have read every...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Books? But you cannot read experience. Inexperience is dangerous. People, left unchecked, will revolt. Walls and borders, unguarded, will be attacked.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Jafar is right. One day, you will understand. You may leave now.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Life will be kinder to you, Princess, once you accept these traditions and understand it's better for you to be seen and not heard.

SCENE.STAGEDIR: A tide that is taking me under
My voice drowned out in the thunder
And I can't start to crumble
To shut me or cut me down
Though they wanna keep me quiet
And I tremble when they try it All I know

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You know what to do, Abu.

SPEECH.LINE: Get off me! Get off me, you dirty monkey! Go away! Go away! Go away! Close the gates!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Iago
SPEECH.LINE: Dirty monkey. Street thief. Street thief.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Iago
SPEECH.LINE: Jafar. If I hear that...! One more time.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Another petty insult from that slow-minded fool. He sees a city where I see an empire.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Once that lamp sits in my hand, then I shall sit on his throne.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Man
SPEECH.LINE: I told you, now, come on! Bigger circles. Bigger circles.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: But Dalia, there has to be something I can do.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: A handsome prince wants to marry you. Oh, when will life get easier?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: It's not that I don't want to marry, it's just...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: You want to be sultan. But why?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: You remember my mother used to say, "We would only ever be as happy as our least happy subject." If she saw what I saw today, she would be heart-broken.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: She would also want you to be safe. And clean. I'll draw the bath.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Jafar's guards on every corner. Soon he'd have them invading our neighbors, risking lives, for what? I can help! I know I can. I was born to do more than marry some useless prince.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: If you had to marry a useless prince, you could certainly do worse than this one. He's tall and handsome, and yes, he's a little dim but, you're just getting married. It's not like you have to talk to him. But you'd prefer that boy from the market.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: You. You! What are you doing here? Get in here, now.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I... came back to return your bracelet.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Not bad! I like what she's done with the place.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: How did you get past the guards?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: That was challenging. But, I have my ways. While the Princess is out, would you like to go for a stroll? Have a little chat?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: You're unbelievable. You cannot just break into a palace and walk around like you own the place.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: If you don't have anything, you have to act like you own everything. So, what do you say? I did find your bracelet.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: You did not find it, you stole it.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Uh... I did. For you, Princess Jasmine.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: Why are you being weird? Oh, I'm... the Princess... Yes! Mm-hmm. And it is good to be me with all my palaces and... wagons of gold... things... and dresses for... every hour of the day. Now it is time for my cat to be cleaned.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Mm-hmm. Clearly. Aren't you supposed to be in the bath?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, Servant girl! This cat isn't going to clean itself.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, okay, but I'm coming back tomorrow night.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Meet me in the courtyard beside the fountain. When the moon is above the minaret. To return this. I promise. Can you believe it, Abu? The most heavily guarded place in all of Agrabah.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Evening. Evening. Guards are behind me. Hello, boys.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: In a world of trouble, boy.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: That bracelet. Is that what this is about? Because I didn't steal it. The handmaid...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: What was a handmaid doing wearing the Queen's bracelet?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: The Queen? No, no. She said that - it belonged to...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Her mother. Well, at least she told the truth about one thing.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: A-Are you saying that was the Princess? I was talking to the...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: She was toying with you. It amuses her to meet commoners. Did you actually think she liked you? What do they call you?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Aladdin. People like us must be realistic.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: You see, I was once like you. A common thief. Only I thought bigger. Steal an apple, and you're a thief. Steal a kingdom, and you're a statesman. Only weak men stop there. You're either the most powerful man in the room, or you're nothing. You. You stumbled upon an opportunity. I can make you rich. Rich enough to impress a princess. But nothing comes for free.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: What would I have to do?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: There's a cave nearby. And in it, a simple oil lamp. Retrieve it for me, and I will make you wealthy enough to impress a princess. You're nothing to her. But you could be. Your life begins now, Aladdin.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: No. No, I came out to... He promised.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: I'll be upstairs if you need me.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: The Cave of Wonders. When you enter, you will see more riches than you've ever dreamed of. Gold, diamonds... and... the lamp. Bring it to me and I will make you rich and free. But take no other treasure, no matter how sorely you are tempted. And you will be tempted.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Cave of Wonders
SPEECH.LINE: Only one may enter here. One who's worth lies far within. A diamond in the rough.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Remember... take nothing but the lamp.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Abu, don't touch. Remember? Whoa. Abu. This is a magic carpet. These really do exist. Hello, carpet. Let’s see what we can do about your situation here. Oh, hey! Don't mention it. Abu, keep your little monkey hands to yourself. Abu! No!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Cave of Wonders
SPEECH.LINE: (voice) You have touched the forbidden treasure! Now, you will never again see the light of day!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Carpet! Abu, jump! Could you give me a hand?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: We don't have much time. Give me the lamp.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Aaahhh! We're alive. I think. Thank you, carpet. Abu. How did you...? You cunning little monkey. Now, all we need's a way out. Hey, carpet, do you know a way out of here? The lamp? What is that?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, great one who summons me. Terrible one who commands me. I stand by my oath. Loyalty to wishes three. I said... oh... great... Excuse me. Boy, where's your boss? Help me out here, where's your boss? If I was gonna talk to myself, I coulda just stayed in the lamp. Hello! Um...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: (Aladdin shudders over trying to speaking shock on seeing the Genie) Hi..!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I'm... talking... to a smoking blue giant?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: No! I am not a giant. I am a genie. There's a difference. Giants are not real. Where's your boss?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Look, kid, I've been doing this a long time, all right? There's always a guy, you know. He's cheated somebody or buried somebody or... I mean, you get my point. Where's that guy?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I know that guy. He's outside.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: So... it's just... you and me down here? And a monkey. That's your personal business, but we gonna need to talk about that monkey later. Such a pretty little monkey. So, you rubbed the lamp?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, okay. Hey, do you mind if I just stretch it out over here? Do you mind?
Aladdin Uh, are you asking me?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No, no, no. You look like you should be my master.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Right, but that's not quite how it works, though.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: How long have you been trapped in here?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: [with Aladdin's head] A thousand years? Kid, is it me, or does, like, everything surprise you? So, you really don't know who I am? Genie, wishes, lamp? None of that ringin' a bell? Wow. Well, th-th-that's a first. Monkey! Oh, you don't need to worry about him, he's fine.
(Genie starts to sing Friend Like Me)

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Well, Ali Baba, he had them forty thieves Scheherazade had a thousand tales
Well, master, you're in luck because up your sleeve You got a genie that never fails! Whoo! I'm the best.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Yeah. Not enough, huh? I'm kidding. Watch this.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie

Well, Ali Baba, he had them forty thieves

Well, I'm on the job, you big nabob
You ain't never had a friend, never had a friend
You ain't never had a friend, never had a friend
You ain't never had a friend like me

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: You can clap now. Puh-domp. No, no, no, no. Please, please, please, please. You can thank me outside. In the sun. When you wish us out.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: You're... kidding, right? The whole song... was the instructions! Obviously you can't dance and listen at the same time. So here's the basics. Step one. Rub the lamp. Step two. Say what you want. Step three. There is no step three. See? It's that easy. You get three wishes. They must begin with rubbing the lamp and saying "I wish". Got it?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: A couple other rules. You can't wish for more wishes. Three is enough. Now, I can't make anybody love anybody. Or bring anybody back from the dead. Feel free to interrupt me anytime you don't understand. I'm kidding. Don't ever interrupt me, no matter what. Now, I usually don't have to go through all this because by the time the guy gets to me, he pretty much knows what he wants and it generally has to do with tons of money and power! Do me a favor, do not drink from that cup. I promise you, there's not enough money and power on Earth for you to be satisfied. Good? So, what's your first wish?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Mm, well... I have to think about it. I mean, if there are only three. Why are there only three, anyway?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: I don't know! Who cares? You don't know.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: That's 'cause you don't listen. I never said I was all-knowing. I said I was all-powerful. The most powerful being in the universe. Look, whatever I don't know, I know I can learn it. Outside in the sun. Why you playin' hard to wish? I know you can't tell, but I'm very pale. This is sky blue. My natural pigmentation is navy. Give us some sun!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Okay, Genie. I wish for you to get us out of this cave.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, yeah! He has made his first wish! Thank you for choosing carpets, camels and caravans. Please don't forget to tip your genie on the way out. Hold yourself, kid! Whoo! Whoa. Man, look at this world. It's so... big. Inside the lamp, everything's like, brass! Brass! Brass! Brass! Like, ooh, is that some copper? Nope! Brass! Sorta the problem with the genie life. Phenomenal cosmic powers, but itty-bitty living space.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: So, is this magic? Or are you magic?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Eh, kind of a package deal.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Can you warn me before you do that?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, you'll get used to it.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Right. So, do I have to make all my wishes here? I mean, if I take you back to Agrabah, won't people...?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: No, no, no, no, no. I can look totally normal.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Right. Totally normal. Still blue. Do we need the top knot?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: That's my little cherry on top.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: So... What are you gonna wish?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, I haven't thought about that.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Mm. You really are not that guy. Okay.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: So what would you wish for?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: No one's ever asked me that before. An easy one though. I wish to be free. To not have to say, Can I help you? What would you like? Welcome to the lamp. May I take your order please? Freedom. I wish to be... To be human.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Why don't you just set yourself free?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Carpet! Did you hear what this boy said? Why don't I set myself free? Only way a genie gets to be free is if the owner of the lamp uses one of his wishes to set him free. And the last time that happened was, like, the fourth of Never-ary.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I'll do it. I've got three, right?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Actually, you have two left. You used one to get out of the cave, remember?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Did I? Or did you? I thought I had to be rubbing the lamp?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Okay, little street boy. Let's rewind the tape. Okay, Genie. Oooh! The old monkey-with-the-lamp trick. Never seen that one before. Keep my eye on you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: At least now I can use my third wish to set you free.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Here's the thing about wishes. The more you have, the more you want.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Uh-oh. Seen that face before. Who is she? Who's the girl?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Aww, aren't they all? Treat your woman like a queen, I always say.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No. No, she's an actual princess.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Well, I already told you. I can't make anyone love anyone, so.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No, no. We had a connection.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: She is smart and kind and so beautiful. But she has to marry a... Hey, can you make me a prince?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: There's a lot of gray area in "make me a prince". I could just... make you a prince.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Right, you'd be snuggled up with that dude for the rest of your life.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Prince
SPEECH.LINE: Yo, you all seen my palace?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Be specific with your words. The deal is in the detail.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Which I don't really understand because if she already likes you, why change?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I told you she has to marry a prince.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Prince
SPEECH.LINE: I just wanna go home, man.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I wish... to become... a prince.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Back up, boy, I need some room to work. I'm about to fabulize you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: A little head-spinning. Better though, right?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I think I'm... getting used to it.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Okay, a prince, a prince. Obviously, the ghetto-chic is not gonna work. I'm feeling a periwinkle.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: No. Chartreuse. The lines are all wrong.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: The color clashes with your skin tone. The silhouette is confused.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: No, that is not a big hat. Come on, Genie, gotta beat the door down. Gotta go bold! Gotta... gold! Aah, no! We've gone too far.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Genie, You're slippin’! It's gotta be neutral for the desert. Ivory, beige, bone, Albion.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Oh! White! And the crowd goes wild! Oho! The Genie! The Genie's on fire! The Genie's on fire, folks. Don't hurt him, Genie! Don't scald him! Somebody cool me down! Somebody put me out! I'm too much! So what do you think?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Of course you like it, boy. I made it.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Strictly speaking, it... it's me. I mean, I did most of the work. You was just standing there. But I hear you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Nobody will recognize you. That's how genie magic works. People see what they're told to see.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Yeah. Like, everybody knows about it. It has a brochure. A little hot in the summer, temperate in the fall. I-I'll let you read it en route.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: How are we gonna get there?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: I guess you can't ride a chimp. Don't you just love a moonwalking monkey? Uh-uh, hold it there, Abu.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Too obvious. We need something robust. Relax, Abu. It's just for a minute.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Guards
SPEECH.LINE: Clear the way! Clear the way!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Crowd
SPEECH.LINE: Make way for Prince Ali! Say hey! It's Prince Ali!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Clear the way in the old bazaar
Oh come, be the first on your block to meet his eye!
Now, try your best to stay calm
Then come and meet his spectacular coterie
A hundred bad guys with swords
Who sent those goons to their lords?
Now the ladies, what he got?
When it comes to exotic-type mammals
There's no question this Ali's alluring
Ali Ababwa Never ordinary, never boring
So, get on out in that square
He's a winner, he's a whiz, a wonder
He's about to pull my heart asunder
To gawk and grovel and stare
He's got ninety-five white Persian monkeys
He got the monkeys, a bunch of monkeys
And to view them he charges no fee
They bow to his whim, love serving him
They're just lousy with loyalty to Ali!
And that, good people, is why
He got all cute and dropped by
With his bears and lions, a brass band and more
His birds that warble on key

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: What you doing? Put your arms down.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: It's a pleasure to welcome you to Agrabah, Prince Ali.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I don't know. It fell off.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: It's a pleasure to meet him.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Uh... It's just as much... a pleasure for me, Your Highness, Sir. You look very serene.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: That's a curtsy, not a bow. Stand up.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: I'm afraid I'm unfamiliar with... Ababwa.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: (Both) It's south... Uh, we have a north and a south.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: It's near, if you were to go... it's... it's...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Around there, you can find it, if you just look.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: The world is changing quickly, Jafar. It seems as if there is a new country every day.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Don't touch me. Don't touch me.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Well, Yes! You are very wise.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Um, tell him that we have gifts, please.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, right! We have things! Gifts! Did you organize this?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Yes, here we are! Gifts. Coming right up. We have spices. Golden camels. And spoons, tiny spoons.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: How do they make 'em that tiny? We have jams!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Yes, jams! Yam jams. Fig jams. Yam jams! And date jams. Seedless. Delicious, exotic jams.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Jewels! We have them. And, uh, that! Over there. Hidden for... suspense. Wfoo. Ta-da.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: And what do you hope to buy with this... expensive?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No! No, no, no, no! A moment with you. A moment... that's not...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Are you suggesting I am for sale?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Of course. Not! No, of course not!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: It's cold, and it's dark in that lamp. But I prefer it to this.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Please excuse me, I need to go and... find some bread.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No, no, that's not what I meant. I...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Just leave her alone. You didn't do great.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: You will get a chance to speak again. We hope you can join us tonight, Prince Ali, when we celebrate our harvest.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Of course, Your Serene Self. We will join you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: In 10,000 years, I've never been that embarrassed.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Don't worry. You are not out of the game yet. Just do whatever you normally do at a party.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I've never been to a party.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, you gonna need to go back to the room then. You're not messin' up my night.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Prince Ali. May I have a word? Privately? This doesn't concern your servant. Be over there, minding my business. I know who you are.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Yes. You're a man of great ambition, like myself.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I, uh, I don't believe we've been properly introduced.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Jafar. Vizier to the Sultan. I could be a valuable ally to help you get what you want.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I, uh, I will think on that. I don't wanna keep the Princess waiting.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Perhaps I'm not making myself clear. This offer will not be on the table for long, you see. Prince Ali...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Excuse me. Prince Ali, your presence is requested. So I guess that's the guy.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Okay, there she is. This is your chance.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You know what? There are a lot of people. I don't think I can do this.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: You'll be fine. Okay. This one's gonna be easy. Ooh! Look at her handmaid. She's gonna get some punch. Feelin' a little thirsty myself.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No. No, no, no, no. You can't leave me. They'll see right through me.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: No, they won't. All you have to do is walk over there.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: And speak. I also have to speak.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Okay, listen to me. I live in a lamp.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: But this is a party. Do not mess this up for me. Okay? I'd like to party. Go get her!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I'm just making sure the water's... a good temperature.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Why are you not over there?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: How am I supposed to compete with that? Look at him. He's so princely.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: You have got to be more confident about what you have to offer.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: What do I have to offer? Knowledge of how to steal food? How to jump between buildings? Now, Prince Ali, he has jewels.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Stop, stop, stop. I made you look like a prince on the outside. I didn't change anything on the inside. Prince Ali got you to the door. But Aladdin has to open it.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Oh. See, the sultan does like me.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Okay, well, maybe he'll let you be his wife.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Look at that. Another prince more interested in Baba.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: I don't know. I think this one might be different. And he comes with an incredibly attractive friend. Please make it work.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, look at him, he's trying so hard.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No, I'm waiting for the right moment.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: No, no. No waiting. We're done waiting.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No. I'm in charge, okay? I say when it's the right moment. Really? Really?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: He's here. Say something. Act natural. Hi!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I'm sorry for the jams. And the jewels, and the-and the... And the buying you. That wasn't me. It, It was me. I don't, It was me. I don't have a twin or anything. But, uh, I... um... - You're...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Something very strange about him. Keep a close eye on Prince Ali.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Maybe I went a tad far with the backflip, but that dance may be the best thing you ever do in your life.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I was pretty good. But nothing seems to impress her.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Yeah, it's crazy. Not gems or jams or jewels. If I can't impress her, I wonder who can.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: I'm just sayin' try being yourself?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: But she has to marry a prince. If I just had a few more minutes with her, I know I could... - You need to get me over there.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No. It's a favor... for a friend.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Ooh, yeah, see, genies don't really have friends.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I thought you said you never had a friend like me?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: No. I said you... never had a friend like me. When you're a genie, somebody always wants somethin' from you. It's awkward.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You're right. You're right. Besides, it would've involved distracting a certain handmaiden...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Ooh! Ha! See what you're doin'. You hooked me. See you there?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: How did you get past the guards?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: All 48 of them? Even the ones that eat fire? Impressive.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: That's why I'm, uh... That's what people say about me. Oh, that-that didn't come out the right way. No one says that. I-I don't know why I said that.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: They're beautiful. She will hate them. Tell Prince Ali, the way to her heart is through her mind.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Actually, these are from me... to you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: Could you excuse me one moment?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Wait, no, just... "That's what people say about me." Come on, Genie!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: My favorites. I accept. Please continue.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: I was noticing how... pleasant the evening is. At the party, I noticed... you noticing how... pleasant I am. Right. How's this going?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: It's clumsy, but in a charming sort of way.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Uh, a stroll. Would you like to take an evening stroll?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: Just the two of us? On purpose?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Okay. Why would I say "as people"? The kid is contagious.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: I've never done this before. How does it work? Do you like sheep cheese?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I just came back... because you left so...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Hmm. Actually, I'm glad you are here.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: I've been trying to find Ababwa, but it doesn't seem to be on any of my maps. Care to show me?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, Rajah, let's not eat the Prince today. He needs his legs for dancing.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Did I go too far with the backflip?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Yes. Genie, I need to find Ababwah.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: The other brother, the short one. Not the one who gifted me a goat.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: She has maps, lots of maps. Ababwah, of course. Ababwah.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: Yak napkins are not easy to find.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: My country? No. No. There it is.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: I don't think so. I've been...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: How did I not see that?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Who needs maps anyway? They're old and useless. And have no practical value.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Maps are how I see the world.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I thought a princess could go anywhere?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Uh, well, uh, would you...? Would you wanna...? Sorry. Sorry.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Thanks... for that. I was saying... You should see these places. I mean, there's a whole world outside of books and maps. Do you want to?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Who said anything about a door?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Sometimes Princess... Sometimes, you just have to take a risk.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: What just happened? What? Is this...?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: A magic carpet. Do you trust me?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I can show you the world Shining, shimmering, splendid Tell me, princess Now when did you last let your heart decide? I can open your eyes Take you wonder by wonder Over, sideways and under On a magic carpet ride A whole new world A new fantastic point of view No one to tell us, "No" Or where to go Or say we're only dreaming

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: A whole new world A dazzling place I never knew But when I'm way up here It's crystal clear That now I'm in a whole new world with you

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Now I'm in a whole new world with you

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Unbelievable sights Indescribable feeling Soaring, tumbling, freewheeling Through an endless diamond sky A whole new world

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Don't you dare close your eyes

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: A hundred thousand things to see

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Hold your breath, it gets better

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: I'm like a shooting star I've come so far I can't go back to where I used to be

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin+Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: I'll chase them anywhere There's time to spare Let me share this whole new world with you

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Of all the places you've shown me, this is by far, the most beautiful.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Sometimes you just have to see it from a different perspective.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: It's them, the people. They make it beautiful. And they deserve a leader who knows that. I don't know why I think it could be me.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Does it matter, what I think?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, look at that adorable monkey down there. Is that Abu?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No, no, it couldn't be Abu, he's...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: So, how many names do you have, Prince Aladdin?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: But how would you know the city so well?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I came to Agrabah early. Because to know a people, you have to see them for yourself. But you already know that. When we first met, you were disguised in your own city. You saw us arrive with dancers, an elephant, and a magic carpet. How could a street thief have all that?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: But how could I not recognize you?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: People don't see the real you when you're royalty.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: I'm sorry. I'm embarrassed. I mean, you've seen more of Agrabah in days than I have in an entire lifetime.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Ooh! I like that face. - Good date?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: But it was better than the best.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: She figured out I was Aladdin. You said that wouldn't happen.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Wow. I mean, genie magic is really just a facade. At some point, the real character's always gonna shine through. But that's a good thing, right? Now, she knows.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: He told me he was only pretending to be a thief to see the city. But he's actually a prince.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I convinced her that I really was a prince.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Eventually, you are going to have to tell her the truth.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Eventually, I will tell her. Anyway, I sort of am a prince now.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Oh. So you're gonna drink from that cup.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: You don't think maybe you want to believe him because you have to marry a prince but you can't marry a thief?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: No. I believe him. I do.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: When the time is right, I will tell her. Morning?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Please, please, I... Gentlemen. I think there's been some sort of confusion. I don't think you know who I am.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, no, no. I know who you are. Aladdin.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I don't know who that is. I'm Prince Ali...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: A prince from a kingdom that doesn't exist. Who now possesses a magic carpet from the Cave of Wonders. Seems to me, the only way that's possible,

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: is if you found a certain treasure. My treasure. Where's the lamp?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: This clearly, is some sort of misunderstanding. I'm Prince Ali.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: If I throw you off of that balcony, and you are who you say you are, you will die a watery death. If you survive, it can only be because of the lamp. In which case, I will have my answer. For the last time... Where is the lamp?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Listen to me. I don't know who you think I am.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Boy, What in the...? Oh! Hey, kid! Okay. I can't get you out of this unless you make a wish. You have to make a wish! Come on, wake up! Okay, try and form the words. "I wish." Kid, come on, wake up! Wake up. You need to think. Think, think. Think. Okay. Okay. A little gray area here, but it's worth a try. I, Aladdin, being of sound body and mind, declare that my second wish is to be saved from certain doom. I took the liberty to backdate that a day. I hope you don't mind. Yes! Here we go! Come on! Come on, kid! Kid! Kid, please. Come on. Wake up. Wake up, wake up. Kid! Wake up, please.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Probably could've done that the other direction though. How you doing?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Hey, no problem, uh, I was in the neighborhood.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I thought you said no favors?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I thought you said no friends?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Right, uh... It actually cost you a wish.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Whatever it cost. You saved my life.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Yeah, that's true. I mean, strictly speaking though, it was a team effort.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Thank you. We have to stop Jafar.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: That's not gonna be easy. The Sultan trusts him completely. He has everybody fooled.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: I overheard Prince Ali talking to his advisor about returning with an army to conquer Agrabah.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Now it appears he's fled in the night.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: You heard him say this, Jafar? And you saw him leave?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Yes. With my own ears and my own eyes. So, what's the problem now, Princess?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: This is the problem, Jafar. If what you say is true, why is Prince Ali still here?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Prince Ali. This is a surprise.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Your Highness, your advisor's not who he says he is.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Oh. I'm not who I say I am.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Baba, he tried to kill Prince Ali.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Jafar, can you explain this to me?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Yes. My Sultan... You know my loyalty belongs to you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Prince Ali, you invited yourself into our city. And we welcomed you as our guest. But I believe your intentions are... deceitful. You are a grave danger to Agrabah, and you shall be dealt with as such.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Enough, Jasmine! Jafar told me of Prince Ali's ambition.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: He's here for my throne. Hakim!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: He had you under a spell.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: He's not to be trusted. Your Majesty.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Jafar, you were my most trusted advisor! Hakim! Put him in the dungeon!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: You should think about how quickly the sands of power shift in the storm, Hakim.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Hakim
SPEECH.LINE: I am loyal to my sultan. As you should have been. The law is the law.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Well, Your Highness, there's something I... I'd like to explain.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Your honor and integrity will never again be questioned here in Agrabah.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: A more noble and sincere young man has never graced the chambers of this palace.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: I would be honored to call you my son.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: If that... were something anyone wanted.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: So you got the girl. I mean, trust me, I had my doubts after your whole jam debacle. But you pulled yourself back together a little bit.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I did, didn't I? I think I finally got the hang of being a prince.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, well, that's not exactly what I meant.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You were right! People see what they wanna see. Aladdin is gone. I'm Prince Ali now.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Wow. Got it all figured out now, huh, kid?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: And you know, I was thinking about my last wish, and I just... I can't do this without you. I know I told you I'd use it to set you free, but you heard the sultan. I can't let everything that we've worked for fall apart.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: So... You just never tell her the truth? You're gonna keep living the lie?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: It's not a lie. People can change.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Heh, oh, they can change, all right.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: And? That's a bad thing? Everything's better now. No one got hurt. You'd rather me be back on the streets stealing to survive? I... thought you'd be happy for me. But all you cared about was that I use my last wish to set you free.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Wow. Kid, I don't care nothin' about that wish. This is about you. What's happening to you. You'd rather lie to somebody you love than give all of this up.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You don't get it, Genie. People like me don't get anything except by pretending.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: I think, maybe, you don't get it. The more you gain by pretending, the less you're actually gonna have. 10,000 years, I have never once, ever... called a master a friend. I broke the rules for you. I saved your life... and for what? You are breaking my heart here, kid. You're breaking my heart.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Who does he think he is? He's supposed to serve me. I'm the same as I always was on the inside, right, Abu?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Aah! Are you kidding? Now, why would you rub the thing under the...? archway.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Riffraff. Street rat. Would they Think that If they look Much closer? Still, I can't play a prince here No, siree. Gotta tell the truth I can't pretend Even if it means This dream will end Even if She walks away From me.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Jafar. You should have left Agrabah while you had the chance.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Why leave when the city belongs to me now?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: I've endured your spineless incompetence for long enough.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Genie! For my first wish, I wish to be Sultan of Agrabah.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Hakim! You obey the sultan. So you obey me now. You know the law, Hakim.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Hakim. Marshal an army to invade Sherabad.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: I think we've heard enough from you, Princess. It's time you start doing what you should've done all along. Stay silent. Guards! Remove her! Control that cat if you know what's good for you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: And understand it's better for you to be seen and not heard. I think we've heard enough from you, Princess. Stay silence.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Written in stone Every rule, every word Centuries old and unbending Stay in your place Better seen and not heard Well, now that story is ending. 'Cause I I cannot start to crumble. So come on and try. Try to shut me and cut me down. I won't be silenced. You can't keep me quiet. Won't tremble when you try it. All I know is I won't go speechless, Speechless. Let the storm in I cannot be broken. No, I won't live unspoken 'Cause I won't go speechless. Try to lock me in this cage. I won't just lay me down and die. I will take these broken wings. And watch me burn across the sky. And it echoes saying I won't be silenced. Though you wanna see me tremble when you try it. All I know is I won't go speechless. Speechless. 'Cause I'll breathe. When they try to suffocate me. Don't you underestimate me. Cause I know that I won't go speechless. All I know is I won't go speechless. Speechless! Hakim! Hakim!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Take her away! Tell them, Hakim.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: You were just a boy when your father came to work the grounds. But you have risen up to become our most trusted soldier. As a man, I know you to be both loyal and just. But now, you have to choose. Duty isn't always honor. Our greatest challenge isn't speaking up against our enemies, but defying those whose approval we seek the most. Jafar is not worthy of your admiration nor your sacrifice.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: I wish nothing but glory for the Kingdom of Agrabah.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: No. You seek glory for yourself. And you would win it off the backs of my people! Hakim. These men, they will follow where you lead, but it's up to you. Will you stand silent while Jafar destroys our beloved kingdom? Or will you do what is right... And stand with the people of Agrabah?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Hakim
SPEECH.LINE: My Princess. Forgive me. My Sultan.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: So this is how it will be. Not even the title of sultan would wake the herd from their sleep. I should have known. If you won't bow before a sultan, you will cower before a sorcerer. Genie! I wish to become the most powerful sorcerer there is.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Hakim. I had such big plans for you. But now... you are no longer of any use to me. Perhaps your men would like to follow you to the dungeon. You too.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: If it isn't our Prince Ali... Ali! Or should I say... Aladdin.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: He's been pretending the entire time. An impostor. There is no Prince Ali. There never was. He's nothing but a lying thief.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: You're insignificant. An irritation I no longer need to tolerate once I... ensure your agonizing death by banishing you to the ends of the earth.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: I could simply kill you all. But that would be inadequate repayment for years of humiliation and neglect. "Remember your place, Jafar." "You forget yourself, Jafar."

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: No. What you need, Baba, is to suffer. Like I have suffered.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Watching me rule your kingdom be enough?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: But watching my armies devour your toothless allies...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: No, the most suitable punishment would be to make you watch - while I take what you love most.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Baba! I will do as you wish! Make it stop! Make it stop. Baba...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Abut, You okay? Let's get out of here. No!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Imam
SPEECH.LINE: Your Highness, Sultan. In honesty and sincerity, do you accept Princess Jasmine...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Imam
SPEECH.LINE: Princess Jasmine, do you accept the Sultan to be your husband?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Abu! Carpet, get me up there! Abu! Jump!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: You are nothing without your staff! Nothing!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Iago
SPEECH.LINE: Punish them. Punish them. Genie should witness.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: As the old man said, “You should have left Agrabah when you had the chance.” I told you before to think bigger. You could have been the most powerful man in the room. But now, I hold the lamp. I hold the power.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You can’t find what you’re looking for in that lamp, Jafar. I tried and failed, and so will you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: You think so? But I am Sultan! I am the greatest sorcerer the world has ever seen. I will create an empire that history cannot ignore. I can destroy cities. I can destroy kingdoms. And I can destroy you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: True. But who made you a sultan? Who made you a sorcerer? There will always be some thing, some man, some being more powerful than you.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Genie gave you your power, and he can take it away.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: For now. But you'll never have more power than the genie. You said it yourself... you're either the most powerful in the room, or you're nothing. You will always be second.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: Second?! Only second? HE SERVES ME! I will make sure no one will ever say these words again! Genie, for my final wish, I wish to become the most powerful being in the universe. More powerful than you!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: A lot of gray area in that wish. But, uh... most powerful being in the universe, coming right up!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: The most powerful in the universe! At last! Second to NO ONE! And I thought I had power before! First, I'll lay waste to those fools in Sherabad.
[as he tries to use his magic to destroy Sherabad, his own shackles appear]

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: What have you done to me?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I haven't done anything to you, Jafar.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: WHAT HAVE YOU DONE TO ME?!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: This was your wish, not mine. A genie might have phenomenal cosmic powers...

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: But an itty-bitty living space. You see, a genie, without a master, goes back in their lamp.
[Jafar's prison lamp appears taking his base]

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: NO! Not the lamp! No, no! I will not forget you, boy. Mark my words, I will not forget what you have done to me!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jafar
SPEECH.LINE: PARROT! YOU'RE COMING WITH ME! [he grabs Iago as he gets sucked into his lamp] No!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: A couple thousand years in the Cave of Wonders ought to cool you off.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Wait... wait. How can I ever thank you?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Oh, no, no. You don't need to thank me. But I hope you accept my apology. I'm sorry... to the both of you. Especially you. You deserve... so much.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: I hope you find what you're looking for.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Ooh, that's a bit of a mess there, Abu. Let me take care of that for you. Beautiful. Ah-ah-ah. No. No long faces, all right? I got an idea, all right? It's your last wish. Royalty was the right idea, okay? We should just rip on that a little bit. All right, here's what I'm thinking. Aladdin, warrior prince! A noble heart in a land where thieves run feral. Jasmine! You like it? No? Okay, all right. I hear you loud and clear. But this is what you need. The governing laws of Agrabah by royal decree. Okay, and... here it is! "Must marry a prince." Right. But, you say the words... and this law just kinda like, goes away. You and the princess are in it together forever.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: You can just make the law disappear?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Psshh. Please. Like it never existed. All right. Last wish. Let's get it.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: I'm ready. Hold on. Here we go.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: What? Oh, Wait. Am I? Wait, wait, wait, um... Tell me to do something.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: ...Get it yourself? Get your own jams!

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: No. Thank you, Genie. I owe you everything. What are you gonna do now?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: Um... Actually, there is this handmaiden that I would love to travel the world with. If she'll have me.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: When do we leave? Also, I want children.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: Lian and Omar, three years apart. They will worship us both and ask lots of questions. And you will entertain them with stories and songs.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Dalia
SPEECH.LINE: A large one with multiple levels and magnificent sails.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Genie
SPEECH.LINE: I was thinking more of a smaller one.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Sit with me, my child. I'm sorry.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: Please, let me finish. I feared losing you. Like I lost your mother. All I saw was my little girl. Not the woman you have become. You have shown me courage and strength. You... are the future of Agrabah. You... shall be the next sultan.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Sultan
SPEECH.LINE: As sultan, you may change the law. He is a good man.

SCENE.TITLE: SCENE 56
SCENE.STAGEDIR: [as Aladdin is walking away from the palace]

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: Stop, thief. Your sultan commands it.

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Aladdin
SPEECH.LINE: Sultan? Does that mean I'm in trouble?

SPEECH.SPEAKER: Jasmine
SPEECH.LINE: [they kiss, then we see them getting married and celebrating their new life together]
